title: Art in Britain and America in the age of revolution
topic_path: Art in Britain and America in the age of revolution

assessment_items.question_content:
**How did Benjamin West's *The Death of General Wolfe* depart from accepted traditions of history painting?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0962faecfc309fc85c9eeeaa3b06f0bf.jpeg)

Benjamin West, *The Death of General Wolfe*, 1770, oil on canvas, 152.6 x 214.5 cm (National Gallery of Canada)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
West departed from conventions of history painting by representing a contemporary event and showing the figures in modern dress. The other answers listed are all typical of history paintings.

[Read this to learn more](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1700-1800-age-of-enlightenment/english-portraiture/a/benjamin-wests-the-death-of-general-wolfe)

choices.content: it idealized the depiction of war
it focused on a contemporary event
it emphasized the death of the central figure
it referenced the death of Christ

assessment_items.question_content:
**What do art historians believe might have been intended for the unfinished space in Gainsborough's *Mr. and Mrs. Andrews*?  **

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d1d11b4531f0802bd9e6f80229e96fd0.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The area around Mrs. Andrew's hands appears unfinished, possibly where Gainsborough had planned to paint a dead bird from the hunt, or a future child.  

[Read this to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1700-1800-age-of-enlightenment/english-portraiture/a/thomas-gainsboroughs-mr-and-mrs-andrews)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Gainsborough's light color palette and treatment of the figure's clothing in his *Portrait of Mr. and Mrs. Andrews* reveal what influence on the artist?  **

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d1d11b4531f0802bd9e6f80229e96fd0.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Gainsborough draws on the French Rococo style in his use of pastel colors and the delicate treatment of his subjects's clothing.  

[Read this to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1700-1800-age-of-enlightenment/english-portraiture/a/thomas-gainsboroughs-mr-and-mrs-andrews)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which painting builds on the historical tradition of trompe l'oeil used in painting?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Peale engaged the viewer through the use of the trompe l'oeil tradition in art, in which the painitng's heighted illusionism fools the eye.

choices.content: Reynolds's *Lady Cockburn and her Three Eldest Sons*

assessment_items.question_content:
**What fueled the market for prints in 17th century Britain? **

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: England experienced economic development in the 18th century, which led to the growth of a wealthy merchant class that lacked the historical prestige of the land gentry.

choices.content: the development of the printing press
the moral decline of the aristocracy

assessment_items.question_content:
**Stuart's approach to portraiture appears most influenced by which of the artists discussed in this tutorial? **

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2471383f888f09bcb0d4023413ed72ea.jpeg)
Gilbert Stuart, *Lansdowne Portrait of George Washington*, 1796, oil on canvas, 96 × 60" / 243.8 × 152.4 cm (National Portrait Gallery)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
In the British Academy, Joshua Reynolds promoted the Grand Manner style that evoked the classical past in order to elevate contemporary subjects. 

[Read this to learn more](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1700-1800-age-of-enlightenment/english-portraiture/a/gilbert-stuarts-lansdowne-portrait)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What detail of Gilbert Stuart's *Landsdowne Portrait* evokes classical traditions of portraiture?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2471383f888f09bcb0d4023413ed72ea.jpeg)
Gilbert Stuart, *Lansdowne Portrait of George Washington*, 1796, oil on canvas, 96 × 60" / 243.8 × 152.4 cm (National Portrait Gallery)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Washington's orator pose and his architectural surroundings reflect Classical traditions of the past. 

[Read this to learn more](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1700-1800-age-of-enlightenment/english-portraiture/a/gilbert-stuarts-lansdowne-portrait)

choices.content: the curtain that frames the background

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why did John Singleton Copley work primarily as a portrait painter? **

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6921f42f5de1755e281700a2905c7a8b.jpeg)
John Singleton Copley, *A Boy with a Flying Squirrel (Henry Pelham)*, 1765, 77.15 x 63.82 cm (Museum of Fine Arts, Boston)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Largely self-taught, Copley experimented with the pose in Boy with a Squirrel to demonstrate his skills in painting more prestigious types of paintings, such as history paintings.

choices.content: They were one of the most prestigious genres of art.
The market for portraiture was strongest among his American patrons.
He had extensive training specializing in portraiture.
It allowed him to experiment with innovative poses and representations of the figure.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is one of the sources for Reynold's *Lady Cockburn and her Three Eldest Sons*? **

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a5d09217da48e040d675b7b5a4758137.jpeg)
Sir Joshua Reynolds, *Lady Cockburn and Her Three Eldest Sons*, 1773, oil on canvas, 55-3/4 x 44-1/2 inches (141.5 x 113 cm) (National Gallery, London)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Reynolds often combined traditions of history painting with portraiture that seemed to elevate his subjects to the status of the classical past.

choices.content: portraits of the sitter's ancestral heritage
a specific moment in the sitters' life

assessment_items.question_content:
**Hogarth's *Marriage à la Mode* offers a social critique through its use of . . . **

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ac2908aa13ba085e49ee81896aad414c.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Hogarth uses ironic content and satirical details to criticize hypocrisy, greed, and the unscrupulous behavior of the upper classes.

choices.content: ancient stories retold in the modern world.

assessment_items.question_content:
**West's treatment of the landscape in The Death of General Wolfe is intended to . . .  **

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/83fed5bfa126b77b96e8fa3d30e4d808.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: West included specific details in the landscape, architecture, and the crouching Native American in order to distinguish the event's occurrence in the New World,

choices.content: highlight characteristics of the New World.
provide an accurate account of this historical event.
reflect the artist's personal love of nature.
